records.display_name: Violations issued once every two hours

payload.claim_text: Violations will be issued once every two hours for illegal double parking and for illegally obstructing bus lanes and bus stops.
payload.description: ACE violations are issued once every two hours per vehicle

payload.claim_text: Obstructing active bus lanes and bus stops and double parking is illegal 24/7.
payload.description: ACE violation applies around the clock

payload.claim_text: Vehicles standing in or obstructing access to bus stops for longer than 2.5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued a violation.
payload.description: Bus stop standing violation threshold

payload.claim_text: Vehicles standing in a bus lane for longer than 5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued an violation.
payload.description: Bus lane standing violation threshold

payload.claim_text: Vehicles that are found to be double parked for longer than 2.5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued a violation.
payload.description: Double parking violation threshold on ACE-enforced roadways

payload.claim_text: On each ACE bus route, the MTA and City issue warning notices for 60 days before fine-bearing violations begin.
payload.description: Warning period policy for ACE enforcement

payload.claim_text: Vehicles committing the above illegal acts for the above specified respective times are issued violations only after being observed by two buses.
payload.description: Violations require observation by two buses

payload.claim_text: All revenue from ACE violations supports MTA operations, including improved bus service.
payload.description: Use of ACE violation revenue

payload.claim_text: ACE violations are civil penalties and do not affect your driving record or insurance rates.
payload.description: Legal classification of ACE violations

payload.claim_text: Each violation is reviewed for accuracy by a City employee before being issued.
payload.description: Accuracy review process for ACE violations

payload.claim_text: Fines start at $50 and increase by $50 for each additional violation received, up to a maximum of $250 per violation.
payload.description: Escalating fine structure for ACE violations

description: A bus-mounted camera system that issues violations to vehicles occupying bus lanes, to double parked vehicles along bus routes, and to vehicles blocking bus stops. Administered in partnership between the MTA, NYC Department of Finance (DOF), and NYC Department of Transportation (DOT). The 2023 NYS Legislature passed a provision in the budget to expand ACE. Currently active on 60 bus routes.

ACE will be implemented in May 2024 as a four-year demonstration. Activated on fourteen routes in May 2024, expand to another 400 buses and fourteen additional routes, then add ten routes per year from 2025 through 2027
Expansion of ACE program beyond bus lanes to vehicles blocking bus stops and double-parked vehicles. Cameras added to up to 1,000 buses and enforced on 28 bus routes.
Expanded scope of ABLE program to also monitor and ticket vehicles obstructing bus stops and double-parked vehicles, following legislative approval.
Expanded scope of ABLE program to include monitoring and ticketing vehicles obstructing bus stops as well as double-parked vehicles, following legislative approval

Expansion of the Automated Bus Lane Enforcement Program (ABLE), renamed ACE. Issues tickets to cars illegally parked in bus stops and bus lanes. Launched June 2024 on 14 existing ABLE routes with state-of-the-art enforcement technology. NYS Budget approved expansion to enforce bus lanes, bus stops, and illegal double parking during a four-year demonstration period.

Activated ACE on 34 routes in partnership with NYC DOT and NYC DOF. As of November 29, all routes transitioned from warning period to issuing violations. Bus lane speeds increased on average by 5%, 20% reduction in collisions, 5-10% estimated reduction in emissions. Covers 1,000 buses.

Expansion of Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) adding 10 new bus routes on September 16, 2024 and another 10 on September 30, 2024. Total reaching 34 routes and over 1,000 ACE-equipped buses across all five boroughs.
